pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **Look, I get why people say Rafe, or "The Noisemaker," has been surprisingly competent, but his reign at the top of the standings is more like finding a twenty-dollar bill on the ground than any actual draft mastery.** You have to feel for Devin, bless his heart, with Spain only managing a 0-0 with Cape Verde, one really has to start asking what the strategy was when Haiti came into the picture — perhaps a bit of wishful thinking? Meanwhile, Gunner, known in these circles as "Bubba G," appears to be having an internal conflict in the matchup between his own France and Senegal, and honestly, it’s like watching someone play chess against themselves and still somehow end up in check. My firm prediction? France will outclass Senegal, but it’ll be like watching a man with two left feet trying to win a dance contest. Now, Zach, or "Mustard Boy," really has his work cut out for him as his beloved Austria faces off against Jordan. I feel for him, truly, but drafting Saudi Arabia as your T4 and expecting miracles is like bringing a mustard packet to a steakhouse and hoping to make a meal of it. In what will be a show of unanticipated mediocrity, Austria will surely overpower Jordan, and in exhilarating fashion, barely scrape by.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner Faces Identity Crisis with France vs Senegal Showdown
pundit_takes.subtitle: Determined to win either way, Bubba G orders two shirts.

pundit_takes.headline: Devin Ready for Iraq Test to Climb WC Pool Rankings
pundit_takes.subtitle: Ghost Pepper confident despite Norway's knack for dizzying strategy changes.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden Looks to Extend Lead with Jordan's Match Against Austria
pundit_takes.subtitle: Backpass Assassin believes unpredictable tactics are better than none.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe Maintains WC Pool Lead Despite Absence of Soccer Knowledge
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker's draft strategy praised as 'unintentionally effective'.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe's teenage dream sits atop while Zach's fantasy crashes to rock bottom.
Gunner relies on Morocco; good luck with finding that on a map!
Gayden crushed son Rafe, proving family dinners are the real battleground.
Model crowned Gunner favorite, but Devin still owning the sim's heart.
